applied_optimizations.name: Triton ReLU kernels
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced PyTorch ReLU with custom Triton kernels for both standalone ReLU after BN1 and fused residual add + ReLU. Using BLOCK_SIZE=4096 for better efficiency on MI355X (wavefront size 64).

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has been killed (exit 137) in all 5 trials without producing any output. The agent appears to spend too long reading files before acting. Need to give it an absolutely minimal, immediate-action

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with mode=default
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton MLIR compilation errors with async_copy_global_to_local on ROCm gfx950

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton BN+ReLU fusion with running stats
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incorrect - harness runs models in training mode, not eval mode
attempted_but_failed.error: Output mismatch due to using running_mean/var instead of batch mean/var

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton batch statistics computation
attempted_but_failed.reason: Nested loops in Triton extremely slow (4x slower than baseline)

attempted_but_failed.name: channels_last memory format
attempted_but_failed.reason: Conversion overhead negated conv speedup for this problem size

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_7
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 7 produced no output (agent crash). Trial 6 achieved 60.30, and we need 62.0. With 38 minutes remaining, one more focused attempt is warranted. The key untried optimization is fusing BN affine +

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_8
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 8 produced no output (agent crash), same as trial 7. Only trial 6 succeeded out of 8 trials. With 38 minutes left, one final attempt is warranted. The best untried optimizations are cudnn.benchm

notes: ResNet BasicBlock is convolution-dominated; elementwise ops are small portion of total time
Triton ReLU gives ~2-3% speedup over PyTorch ReLU
Further optimization would require Triton convolutions which are extremely complex on ROCm
Best approach: use PyTorch convs+BN (MIOpen) + Triton for elementwise fusion

global_notes: Implementation uses Triton for ReLU operations while keeping convolutions and BatchNorm in PyTorch
